{"key_event":"Wartime centralization and Quit India repression","reasoning":"Civil society remained highly mobilized and organizationally broad—Congress, the Muslim League, unions, peasants, students, and INA symbolism all mattered—but emergency repression, censorship, mass arrests, famine disruption, and communal polarization reduced its unified capacity to constrain the Raj. The colonial state’s coercive and administrative reach expanded through wartime controls, intelligence, army growth, and economic intervention, though famine misgovernance and loss of legitimacy keep it below overwhelming effectiveness.","society_change":-0.5,"society_power":6.8,"state_change":0.3,"state_power":8.8}
